skill.name: agentic-structure
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents through collaborative planning, secure implementation, error handling, comments, discussions, and knowledge-gap workflows for maintainable software.
skill.summary: A collaborative coding framework for safer planning, implementation, review, and documentation workflows.
skill.author: BackGwa

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high patterns, but review found them to be documentation false positives, mostly Markdown backticks, security terminology, and examples that warn against unsafe practices. No prompt injection, credential access, malware behavior, or command execution intent was found. The remaining low risk is that the knowledge protocol describes creating reference files and using web fetches for documentation.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Reference Workflow Requires Awareness
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create and update reference Markdown files under references/[topic]. This is a legitimate documentation workflow, not malicious persistence. Risk is low because paths are scoped and no sensitive files are targeted. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: Direct file creation guidance appears in the knowledge protocol, but the context is documentation for user-provided references and cleanup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file creation guidance appears in the knowledge protocol, but the context is documentation for user-provided references and cleanup.

low_findings.title: Web Reference Workflow Requires Source Review
low_findings.description: The skill tells agents to use web fetching when users provide URLs or when technical information is needed. This is a bounded research workflow, not data exfiltration. Risk is low because it does not direct sending secrets or private code to external services. Confidence: 0.80. Confidence reasoning: The network-related guidance is explicit, but its purpose is retrieving and documenting external technical references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network-related guidance is explicit, but its purpose is retrieving and documenting external technical references.

low_findings.title: Static Critical and High Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM and weak cryptography alerts do not indicate credential access or unsafe cryptography. The reviewed lines are guideline links, security advice, or recommendations against MD5, SHA-1, and raw SHA-256 for passwords. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The cited files contain prose and security best practices, with no executable code or instructions to access credential stores.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files contain prose and security best practices, with no executable code or instructions to access credential stores.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown and Tool-Name False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick execution alerts are caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, and references to Read, Grep, Glob, and WebFetch. No shell execution command or arbitrary command template was found. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The matched backticks wrap documentation examples and filenames, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched backticks wrap documentation examples and filenames, not executable Ruby or shell code.

content.value_statement: AI coding sessions can drift without shared process, security checks, and clear decision points. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical framework for collaborative, production-minded development.
actual_capabilities: Selects relevant development guidelines for planning, coding, comments, errors, security, and knowledge gaps.
Encourages core logic before integration and interface work.
Provides security defaults for secrets, hashing, authorization, rate limits, uploads, and production exposure.
Defines when to ask users for decisions, missing requirements, or domain knowledge.
Guides high-signal comments and consistent error handling across layers.
Documents external references when technical information must be preserved.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not implement code by itself.
It may require user input when requirements or architecture choices are unclear.
Its file and web reference workflow depends on tool permissions in the host agent.
It does not replace project-specific security review or tests.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature Safely
use_cases.description: Break a feature into core logic, integration, and interface steps before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI Coding Reviews
use_cases.description: Use shared criteria for security, error handling, comments, and maintainability during code review.

use_cases.title: Manage Unknown Requirements
use_cases.description: Identify knowledge gaps, ask focused questions, and document references before coding speculative behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Start With Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentic-structure to plan this feature. Identify core logic, integration work, interface work, risks, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Code Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply agentic-structure to review this change for maintainability, comments, error handling, and security assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Ambiguity
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentic-structure to identify unclear requirements, present two or three implementation options, and recommend one with reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply agentic-structure to evaluate this production workflow for secrets handling, authorization, rate limits, failure modes, and validation coverage.

output: Core logic: token creation, expiry, verification, and password update rules.
Integration: email delivery, rate limits, audit logging, and server-side authorization checks.
Validation: expired token, reused token, unknown account, and repeated request cases.
The review separates recoverable dependency failures from user validation failures.
It recommends preserving operator context while keeping user-facing errors generic.
It flags broad catch blocks that hide failure details from callers.

output_examples.input: The API documentation is missing for a third-party provider.
output: The response identifies the missing contract details.
It asks for official documentation or user-provided behavior notes.
It records the reference source before implementation continues.

best_practices: Use the guideline selection matrix before starting broad coding tasks.
Run validation after each logical implementation step.
Document security assumptions when code cannot express them clearly.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the guidelines as a substitute for project requirements.
Do not create reference files for information already visible in the codebase.
Do not use broad error handling that hides useful failure context.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It gives AI coding agents a structured workflow for planning, coding, security, errors, comments, and requirement discussions.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No executable scripts are included. The reviewed files are Markdown guidance documents.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The static alerts were false positives, but the skill describes web research and reference file workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with security-sensitive work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for secrets, hashing, authorization, rate limiting, uploads, and production exposure.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when starting features, reviewing code, handling errors, clarifying requirements, or documenting missing technical context.

faq.question: Does it replace tests or security review?
faq.answer: No. It improves process discipline, but project-specific tests and human review are still required.
